title: Fixtures, Golden Files, Properties, Fuzzing, HTML Validation, and Browser Tests · Independent Program Trial
summary: An independent documentation team receives a different content tree, language, route base, template, asset set, trust policy, hosting target, and accessibility need and transfers fixtures, golden files, properties, fuzzing, html validation, and browser tests into a changed layered parser-to-browser verification matrix. Planned artifact: Produce an independent changed-context version of layered parser-to-browser verification matrix with automated and judgment evidence. Learners progress through…
objectives: Separate scanner, parser, renderer, route, asset, incremental, CLI, filesystem, validation, browser, accessibility, deployment, and recovery evidence.
Use small reviewed input, expected tree, diagnostics, route manifest, and output fixtures with explicit update review.
Check consumption, round-trip boundaries, tree validity, escaping, route uniqueness, determinism, clean equivalence, and idempotence across generated cases.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-static-site-python cumulative production static-site generator portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python parser, route, render, graph, and publication models
with original fixed in-memory fixtures only. Native filesystems, full CommonMark, markdown-it-py,
Jinja, validators, browsers, GitHub Actions, Pages, DNS, TLS, CDN caches, and production effects
require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class PublicationPolicy:
    base_path: str = "/handbook/"
    encoding: str = "utf-8"
    raw_html_allowed: bool = False
    max_document_bytes: int = 262_144
    max_nesting_depth: int = 64


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class BuildEvidence:
    revision: str
    content_manifest_digest: str
    route_manifest_digest: str
    artifact_digest: str
    accessibility_review_id: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python static-site generator portfolio ready"

steps.title: Static-site publishing and Python Commit to the mission model · Lab Test Pyramid
steps.instruction:
lab case — A lab publishing team handles case 1715 during an accessibility-led community newsroom release. Build deterministic Python evidence to combine mutation-sensitive parser, property, validation, and browser evidence; reconcile content, routes, navigation, links, assets, feeds, sitemaps, output files, and artifact digests before approval; then delete a source and change a shared layout, then compare incremental and clean artifact digests. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real filesystems, full CommonMark, markdown-it-py, Jinja, validators, browsers, GitHub Actions, Pages, DNS, TLS, CDN caches, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates.

What must be established before implementation?

Evidence target: Static-site publishing and Python Commit to the mission model · Lab Test Pyramid.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for separate scanner, parser, renderer, route, asset, incremental, cli, filesystem, validation, browser, accessibility, deployment, and recovery evidence. For Static-site publishing and Python Commit to the mission model · Lab Test Pyramid, keep Test Pyramid connected to executable Python source, parse, route, render, accessibility, artifact, deployment, and recovery decision evidence.

content.code:
# Evidence: ssg-validation-browser-zvssoa
# Competency: ssg-validation-browser.
# Case 5123: an accessibility-led community newsroom release.
# Operating constraint: retain reader, content owner, revision, configuration, source, parser, route, artifact, deployment, and result identity.
# Changed case: delete a source and change a shared layout, then compare incremental and clean artifact digests.
def evidence_ssg_validation_browser_zvssoa(fixture_cases: int = 48, detected_mutants: int = 46, required_mutants: int = 45, validator_errors: int = 0, browser_tasks_failed: int = 0) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_zvssoa = "zvssoa-aossvz-5123"
    assert evidence_variant_zvssoa
    evidence_axes_zvssoa = (
        "readerzvssoa",
        "source5123",
        "parseaossvz",
        "routezvssoa5123",
        "artifact5123zvssoa",
        "recoveryaossvz5123",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_zvssoa)) == 6
    if fixture_cases < 10 or detected_mutants < 0:
        return False, detected_mutants, "test-shape"
    if detected_mutants < required_mutants:
        return False, detected_mutants, "mutation-gap"
    if validator_errors > 0:
        return False, validator_errors, "invalid-output"
    if browser_tasks_failed > 0:
        return False, browser_tasks_failed, "reader-flow-failure"
    assert detected_mutants >= required_mutants
    return True, detected_mutants, "layered-verification"
